commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR hoists the interactive session-store lookup, passes its entry session UUID into the diagnostic message lifecycle, and adds a Slack dispatch regression test for diagnostic session IDs.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +33. Total +34 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection on the PR head: native commands can resolve the store entry from `CommandTargetSessionKey` while the lifecycle default `sessionKey` still comes from `ctx.SessionKey`. I did not run the Vitest suite because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can align the lifecycle's diagnostic ref defaults and add the missing native command-target regression without a product decision.

target.title: fix(diagnostics): carry session UUID on interactive dispatch events
target.body:
## Why

Plugins and exporters that subscribe to diagnostic events via `onDiagnosticEvent` need the canonical session UUID — the same id OpenClaw's JSONL session transcript writes as its session-header `id` field (`src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/bootstrap.ts`) — so downstream systems can join diagnostic/telemetry rows back to the transcript by id.

The **cron / isolated-agent** path already provides it: `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts` passes `sessionId: prepared.context.runSessionId` into `createDiagnosticMessageLifecycle`, so `message.queued` and `session.state` events carry the UUID.

The **interactive dispatch** path does not. `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` creates the diagnostic lifecycle with `sessionKey` only — even though the canonical UUID is available from the session store entry (`resolveSessionStoreLookup(ctx, cfg).entry?.sessionId`, the same source already used a few lines later to resolve `operationSessionId`). As a result every interactive `message.queued` / `session.state` event emits `sessionId: undefined`, and consumers cannot join those events to the transcript by UUID.

This is an asymmetry/oversight, not a design choice — there is no config that turns it on.

## Change

Pass `sessionId: initialSessionStoreEntry.entry?.sessionId` into the interactive `createDiagnosticMessageLifecycle({...})` call. The `resolveSessionStoreLookup(ctx, cfg)` lookup (read-only; needs only `ctx` + `cfg`, both already in scope) is hoisted a few lines up so its result is available at lifecycle-creation time. The lifecycle's existing `resolveRef` then defaults `sessionId` onto every emitted event (`message.queued`, `session.state`, `message.processed`), matching the cron path.

Safety: when the store entry exists, `entry?.sessionId` equals the UUID written to the JSONL session header. When it is absent (brand-new session not yet persisted), it is `undefined`, which preserves today's `sessionKey`-only behavior — the change never emits a *mismatched* UUID. This mirrors the existing canonical resolution (`operationSessionId = ... ?? initialSessionStoreEntry.entry?.sessionId ?? ...`).

## Files

- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` — hoist `initialSessionStoreEntry` lookup above the lifecycle creation; pass `sessionId` (+2/−1).
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — new test asserting interactive diagnostic events carry the session-store UUID.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** On the interactive dispatch path, diagnostic events (`message.queued`, `session.state`, `message.processed`) were emitted with `sessionId: undefined`, so a plugin subscribing via `onDiagnosticEvent` could not join them to the JSONL session transcript by UUID. After this patch they carry the canonical session UUID, matching the cron path.

**Real environment tested:** A deployed OpenClaw gateway (v2026.4.29) built from this branch on a Linux host, running a live Telegram channel with an `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` agent. Diagnostic spans exported via OTLP through a running OpenTelemetry collector into Grafana Tempo.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built the runtime from this branch into the gateway `dist/`, restarted the gateway service, sent a real message to the agent over Telegram (session `agent:main:main`), then queried the trace store with `curl`:
`curl -sG "$TEMPO/api/search" --data-urlencode 'q={ .prov.harness="openclaw" && .prov.session.uuid != "" }'`, then fetched the returned trace and read its span attributes. Gateway events were read live with `journalctl -u openclaw-gateway -f`.

**After-fix evidence:** Live runtime output (redacted). The interactive turn ran its full lifecycle in the gateway log:
```
[agentweave-bridge] event: message.queued      sessionKey: agent:main:main source: dispatch
[agentweave-bridge] event: model.call.started  sessionKey: agent:main:main
[agentweave-bridge] event: model.call.completed sessionKey: agent:main:main
[agentweave-bridge] event: model.usage         sessionKey: agent:main:main
[agentweave-bridge] event: message.processed    sessionKey: agent:main:main
```
The resulting `openclaw.turn` span, fetched from Tempo (`curl .../api/traces/<id>`), now carries the UUID — it carried none on this path before the patch:
```
span: openclaw.turn
  prov.harness      = openclaw
  prov.session.id   = agent:main:main                       (route key, unchanged)
  prov.session.key  = agent:main:main
  prov.session.uuid = 4767da88-20d1-459d-b1d6-60ecd493b0fa   (canonical session UUID, now present)
```
`4767da88-…` is the same UUID OpenClaw writes to the JSONL session-header `id`, so the diagnostic stream / span can now be joined to the transcript by id.

**Observed result after fix:** The interactive turn completed normally and its diagnostic events + telemetry span carry `sessionId` / `prov.session.uuid` equal to the canonical session UUID. Before the patch this field was absent on the interactive path; `prov.session.id` (the route key) is unchanged, so parent-linking is unaffected. No other behavior changed.

**What was not tested:** The brand-new-session case where no store entry exists yet — the lookup returns `undefined` and the event keeps today's `sessionKey`-only behavior. That path is covered by the added unit test but was not exercised against the live gateway.

## Tests

New test `carries the session store UUID on interactive diagnostic events`: drives a real interactive (Slack) dispatch with a store entry whose `sessionId` is `test-uuid-1234`, and asserts the emitted `logMessageQueued` and `logSessionStateChange` both carry `sessionId: "test-uuid-1234"`. Red before the change, green after. Full `dispatch-from-config.test.ts` suite remains green. (Supplemental to the live proof above.)

## Scope

Independent of #80497 (which adds `onModelDiagnosticEvent` at the delivery/filter layer in `src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts`). This PR is at the emit/population layer (`dispatch-from-config.ts`) and touches disjoint files; the two can land in either order.
target.author: arniesaha

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
